Environmental justice considerations mandate equitable enforcement of air quality regulations across all communities.

Inadmissible individuals may seek admission through waiver applications demonstrating qualifying hardship to United States citizen relatives.
Expedited removal procedures apply at ports of entry for individuals lacking proper documentation.

Waste-to-energy facilities must comply with air emission standards and demonstrate adequate pollution control technology.
Contribution claims allow responsible parties to seek reimbursement from other potentially liable parties for cleanup expenditures.

Fire prevention protocols require implementation of approved safety measures during all forest operations.
Environmental monitoring protocols must be implemented to assess the ecological health of managed forest areas.

Agencies retain discretion to negotiate consent decrees that establish compliance schedules and remedial measures.
Clean Air Act violations may result in civil penalties of up to twenty-five thousand dollars per day per violation.
